ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotani biribis nwa': Hori nɛ Hemam. Timna da anɛ Lotani taʋn.
translated: The names of Lotani's children are: Hori and Hemam. Timna was also Lotani's wife.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Ka Esau gɔs ka nyɛ pu'ab nɛ biis la, ka bu'os o ye, “Anɔ'ɔnamɛ nwana dɔlli fɔ?” Ka Jakob lɛbis ye, “Bibama ka Wina'am nɔki tisim, m bierɛ.”
translated: Esau asked his father for food, and he said to him, "What is this that you have hunted so quickly?" Jacob replied, "Because the Lord your God brought it to me."

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: ka bu'os sankpan'as la ye, “Dau kanɛ yi sian'arin kenna tʋ'ʋsidi ti la aan anɔ'ɔnɛ?” Ka sankpan'as la lɛbis ye, “O anɛ m zugdaan.” Ka Rebɛka nɔk fubili lus o mɛŋ.
translated: The old man asked the elder, "How is it that you have lived long and your body is still strong?" The elder replied, "It is because I am patient." And Rebekah kept these words in her heart.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Nɔnaar la nya'aŋ la ka Abimelek nɛ Fikol onɛ an o soogianam kpɛɛm la lɛbi kul Filistia sʋ'ʋlimin.
translated: Abimelek and Phicol the commander of his forces said to Abraham, "God is with you in everything you do."

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ka o yɛl o ye, “Kem gɔs ye ban nɛ pɛ'ɛs la wʋsa mɔr laafi, ka lɛb na ka m wʋmi ba labaar.” Ka o tʋm o ka o yi Hebron bɔn'ɔgin la keŋ hali ti paae Sekɛm teŋin.
translated: And he said to him, "Go and see if your brothers are well, and the flock, and bring word back to me." And he sent him from the valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
